rationale: The citing paper uses He, Inman, and Mittal (2008) to support a core theoretical premise in its hypothesis development: gender-linked agentic versus communal orientation shapes how men and women make economic decisions. The citation is not just a perfunctory literature list entry; it appears in the conceptual table and in the argument that men are more self-focused while women incorporate others, which underpins the proposed payment-motive mechanism. This prior evidence helps justify transferring the orientation-based logic into PWYW pricing behavior.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the gender-and-orientation logic from He et al. (2008) to a new decision domain, pay-what-you-want pricing. It uses that prior finding as conceptual support for hypotheses about gender differences in social versus economic payment motives and resulting payments.
